summary: Backend-only verification of the payment-gate removal in the driver marketplace endpoints. Wrote 7 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_marketplace_visibility.py; all 7 PASSED. /api/orders/available now returns 34 pending orders (29 'unpaid' + 5 'pending' payment_status), 8 of which are shipper-created. /api/orders/pending returns a real order object (id=55897bfd... payment_status=unpaid) instead of null. /api/orders/available/matched returns 10 matched pending jobs for the demo driver and still enforces auth (401/403 without token). Fix is working as expected.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~6400 lines — should be split into modules (orders, drivers, shippers, payments, admin) per the 700-line guideline.

/api/orders/pending has no driver auth and returns the first pending order found in collection order; OK for current consumers but consider documenting that it is unauthenticated and not driver-scoped.
seed_data_creation: No new seed created; existing demo driver (demo.driver@nadaruns.com) and 34 pending orders were already in DB. Fixture falls back to POST /api/seed-demo if login fails.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Marketplace visibility fix is verified at the API level. 34 pending orders are visible to drivers, all with payment_status unpaid/pending — confirms the gate is removed. If a frontend retest is requested later, sign in as demo.driver@nadaruns.com / demo1234 and confirm the driver map shows pending jobs.
rca_of_issue: Previous code on get_available_orders and get_pending added a filter payment_status: {$in: [authorized, captured]}. Since shipper-created jobs default to payment_status='unpaid' until Stripe authorization, the marketplace was empty/short. Removing those two filter clauses (per user option 1a) restores immediate visibility. Verified live: 34 orders returned post-fix, all with unpaid/pending payment_status.
